## Smart Textiles Market Analysis and Size

Smart textiles and interactive fabrics have gained immense popularity for personal health management due to the integration of energy sources, [sensors](https://www.databridgemarketresearch.com/reports/global-sensors-market)/ actuators, and communication utilized for personal applications such as protection/safety, healthcare, and emergency. Smart textiles, also known as e-textiles, are fabrics integrated with digital components such as sensors, [microcontrollers](https://www.databridgemarketresearch.com/reports/global-microcontroller-market), and conductive fibers. These textiles can sense, react, and adapt to environmental conditions or stimuli from mechanical, thermal, or chemical sources. Unlike traditional fabrics, smart textiles offer interactive functionalities and can communicate data. They are increasingly being used in various sectors including healthcare, sports, military, and fashion. In healthcare, they can monitor vital signs and alert medical professionals. In sports, they can track performance metric

Global smart textiles market was valued at USD 2.80 billion in 2022 and is expected to reach USD 24.84 billion by 2030, registering a CAGR of 31.3% during the forecast period of 2023-2030. “Sports and Fitness” accounts for the largest end users segment in the respective market owing to the high use in sophisticated electronics devices including fitness watches and belts.  In addition to the insights on market scenarios such as market value, growth rate, segmentation, geographical coverage, and major players, the market reports curated by the Data Bridge Market Research also include in-depth expert analysis, geographically represented company-wise production and capacity, network layouts of distributors and partners, detailed and updated price trend analysis and deficit analysis of supply chain and demand.

**Market Definition**

A textile is defined as an object which is made by weaving of threads or yarns into a fabric originally made of natural animal or plant-based fibers. With the rise in technological advancement, these started being manufactured in laboratories resulting in stronger and more versatile output products. Now, thin fibers run through your smartphone and relay data can be woven into cloth textiles due to the digital communications. Smart textiles refer to cloth which consists of modern computer-based technology woven into it.

## Global Smart Textiles Market Dynamics

**Drivers**

* **Increase in Broadband Enabled Devices**

The increase in the adoption of the semiconductors for the broadband enabled devices and broadband applications acts as one of the major factors driving the smart textiles market. The surge in demand for the consumer electronics devices along with the expansion of wearable electronics industry drives the market further.

* **Technological Advancements**

The continuous technological advancements in the smart textiles along with lower power consumption accelerate the market growth. The surge in the popularity of the technology owing to its characteristics such as added convenience, increased productivity, and better reliability and quality positively impact the market.

* **Internet of Things (IoT)**

The rise in the applications of the Internet of Things (IoT) including [smart homes](https://www.databridgemarketresearch.com/reports/global-smart-home-market), [smart cities](https://www.databridgemarketresearch.com/reports/global-smart-cities-market), Machine-to-Machine (M2M) communications, and Industrial IoT (IIoT), among various others further influence the growth of the market.

Additionally, rapid urbanization, change in lifestyle, the surge in investments and increased consumer spending positively impact the global smart textiles market**.**

**Opportunity**

* **Development of Flexible Electronics and Emergence of Hybrid Application**

Furthermore, development of flexible electronics and emergence of hybrid application and multi-featured mobile devices extend profitable opportunities to the market players in the forecast period of 2023 to 2030. The rise in applications of product in the wearables will further expand the market.

**Restraint/Challenge**

* **High Production Cost**

High production cost owing to requirement of high-end materials and lack of standards and regulations are expected to obstruct market growth. Also, the rise in concerns associated with thermal consideration and product protection and technical difficulties associated with integration of electronics and textiles are projected to challenge the global smart textiles market in the forecast period of 2023 to 2030.

# Smart Fabrics Market Size, Share & Trends Analysis Report By Product (Passive, Active, Very Smart), By Functionality (Sensing, Energy Harvesting, Luminescence & Aesthetics), By End-use, By Region, And Segment Forecasts, 2023 - 2030

## **Smart Fabrics Market Summary**

**The global smart fabrics market size was estimated at USD 2.48 billion in 2022 and is projected to reach USD 21.85 billion by 2030, growing at a CAGR of 31.3% from 2023 to 2030.** This growth is driven by factors such as increasing demand for wearable technology, advancement in textile manufacturing, expanding industrial & military applications, and rising traction in healthcare applications.

### Key Market Trends & Insights

### Market Size & Forecast

Smart textiles/fabrics are advanced fabrics developed with novel technologies that offer the wearer added value. Some fabrics also accumulate energy from the surroundings by extracting vibrations, heat, or sound, responding to these inputs. Such textiles are used across several applications to enhance performance and add aesthetic value. For instance, in July 2021, Nextiles, Inc. launched a smart fabric software development kit (SDK), which uses smart fabric to record human movement data. It develops smart fabrics with biomechanic and biometric sensing capabilities by fusing conventional sewing methods with printed circuit boards.

![China Smart Fabrics market size and growth rate, 2023 - 2030](/static/img/research/china-smart-fabrics-market.png "China Smart Fabrics Market size and growth rate, 2023 - 2030")

![China Smart Fabrics market size and growth rate, 2023 - 2030](/static/img/research/china-smart-fabrics-market.png "China Smart Fabrics Market size and growth rate, 2023 - 2030")

Until now, most smart fabrics have been limited to niche usage, including the high-end fashion, military & defense, and medical sectors. However, now the industry is witnessing a shift with a positive confluence of technology and textiles that have led to the easy integration of comfortable fabric-based sensors together with the advent of conductive yarns that can be woven into fabrics. Smart textiles are also anticipated to revolutionize the smart wearables market.

Developing clothes/fabrics that monitor every movement with groundbreaking technology, which allows the recycling and reuse of textiles, is expected to play a key role in driving the market over the forecast period. Technological advancements in the wireless and electronics segment have led to the miniaturization and assimilation of electronic components in conductive textiles, thereby spurring market growth. The rise in the Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) technology integration that offers features such as low power consumption and multi-vendor interoperability will also contribute to the growth.

Attires enabled with BLE technology can feel, sense, and regulate data, provided the device has an internet connection. The increasing popularity of sophisticated gadgets with various technologically advanced functions, including sensing and reacting to the surroundings, is expected to drive the market. Components, such as actuators, controllers, and sensor units, are incorporated into these fabrics for proper functioning. For protection and safety, consumers & military professionals use smart textiles/fabrics as a clothing entity.

Furthermore, decreasing the manufacturing costs of electronic components is expected to fuel the market over the forecast period. A rise in demand can also be attributed to the introduction of conductive materials and advanced fibers used to manufacture such fibers. [Nanotechnology](https://www.grandviewresearch.com/industry-analysis/nanotechnology-market-report) has helped in the creation of such intelligent fabrics at a microscopic level and also in the development of army uniforms and equipment.

The COVID-19 pandemic had a significant negative impact on the smart fabrics market. Restrictions on international trade, factory closures, and limitations on transportation have caused delays in the supply of raw materials and finished products. The economic uncertainties resulting from the pandemic have led to reduced consumer spending on non-essential items, including smart fabrics. On the other hand, the pandemic has also created new opportunities for smart fabrics in healthcare and hygiene-related applications. The demand for antimicrobial and self-cleaning fabrics, as well as wearable health monitoring devices has increased. Smart fabrics that offer features like virus filtration and temperature monitoring have gained attention in the healthcare sector.

## **Functionality Insights**

Based on functionality, the global market has been segmented into sensing, energy harvesting, luminescence & aesthetics, thermoelectricity, and others. The sensing segment accounted for the largest market share of 37.3% in 2022. It is attributed to the ability to sense, which is the most essential attribute of any smart garment. These fabrics are embedded with sensors that can detect and measure various physical attributes such as temperature, pressure, moisture, and movement. They are used in sports and fitness monitoring, healthcare, and military applications.

![Global Smart Fabrics market share and size, 2022](/static/img/research/global-smart-fabrics-market.png "Global Smart Fabrics market share and size, 2022")

![Global Smart Fabrics market share and size, 2022](/static/img/research/global-smart-fabrics-market.png "Global Smart Fabrics market share and size, 2022")

On the other hand, the energy harvesting segment is projected to register the fastest CAGR of 32.0% over the forecast period. These fabrics are designed to capture and store energy from the surrounding environment, such as solar or kinetic energy. The stored energy can power electronic devices or sensors integrated into the fabric.

## **Regional Insights**

North America dominated the market and held the largest share of 48.4% in 2022 and is also projected to be the fastest-growing regional market. North America is home to several prominent companies operating in the smart fabric industry. These companies are developing, manufacturing, and marketing smart fabric-based products, contributing to the market's growth. The presence of established players helps drive product innovation and market competitiveness. The region has a robust technological infrastructure and expertise, facilitating advancements in smart fabric technologies.

![Smart Fabrics Market Trends, by Region, 2023 - 2030](/static/img/research/smart-fabrics-market-trends-by-region.png "Smart Fabrics Market Trends, by Region, 2023 - 2030")

![Smart Fabrics Market Trends, by Region, 2023 - 2030](/static/img/research/smart-fabrics-market-trends-by-region.png "Smart Fabrics Market Trends, by Region, 2023 - 2030")

Asia Pacific is also anticipated to witness steady growth during the forecast period. The availability of low-cost raw materials and labor in Asian countries, such as China, Japan, and India, has transformed it into a manufacturing hub for such technology. It has led to a decline in the European region's overall production of smart fabrics. However, an increasing number of R&D activities for such intelligent fabrics sponsored within the EU FP6 and FP7 programs are revitalizing the industry in European countries.

## **Product Insights**

The active segment held the largest market share of 42.8% in 2022. The active segment includes sports and fitness activities fabrics, such as workout clothes, sports bras, fitness bands, and running shoes. Smart fabrics offer several advantages in this segment, including enhanced performance, comfort, and monitoring capabilities. Smart fabrics in the active arena often incorporate technologies like moisture-wicking, temperature regulation, and compression. These features help improve athletic performance by keeping the wearer dry, cool, and comfortable during workouts. Smart fabrics can also enhance flexibility and movement, providing a better range of motion.

The very smart product segment is expected to expand at the fastest CAGR of 37.7% over the forecast period. It is attributed to continuous advancement in sensor technology, conductive material, and miniaturization of electronic components attached to smart fabrics.

Passive smart fabrics are wearable clothing that can sense the user's movement and environment. The segment is expected to expand significantly during the forecast period due to low product prices and less complex functionalities than other variants. The very smart textile can sense, react, and adapt its behavior to the given circumstances and follows the [Artificial Intelligence](https://www.grandviewresearch.com/industry-analysis/artificial-intelligence-ai-market) (AI) principle. Also, its ability to effectively deal with life-threatening circumstances, such as accidents or to maintain high levels of comfort even during extreme environmental changes is anticipated to fuel its growth.

## **End-use Insights**

Based on end-use, the defense & military segment accounted for the largest revenue share of 28.2% in 2022. The defense & military sectors are exploring the use smart fabrics for applications such as soldier protection systems, body monitoring, and communication. Smart fabrics' lightweight and flexible nature makes them suitable for these industries, where mobility and comfort are crucial. ## **Recent Developments**

In April 2023, researchers in the UK achieved a significant development by producing the world's inaugural smart fabric capable of transforming its shape and color in response to two distinct stimuli: heat and electricity. This breakthrough development unlocks many possibilities in numerous domains, including virtual reality and robotics. The fabric comprises stimuli-responsive materials (SRMs) that react to external stimuli such as light, temperature, magnetic fields, or electricity. When exposed to these stimuli, the SRM undergoes molecular composition, mechanical properties, or shape alterations. This advancement paves the way for innovative applications and advancements in responsive textiles.

In April 2023, researchers at the University of Waterloo created a groundbreaking smart material capable of responding to two distinct stimuli: heat and electricity. This remarkable development marks the first-ever material with such dual responsiveness. This smart material's innovative design opens up many possibilities for various applications. For instance, it could be utilized in clothing that generates warmth as one walks from the car to the office during winter. Additionally, it could be employed in vehicle bumpers that regain their original shape following a collision. These examples demonstrate the broad potential of this unique material in enhancing everyday experiences and advancing technology.

In February 2023, a collaborative effort between researchers at Finland's Aalto University and the University of Cambridge resulted in the development of innovative textiles capable of changing their shape in response to temperature variations. These responsive smart fabrics offer customizable aesthetics and hold the potential for monitoring people's health, improving thermal insulation, and providing tools for controlling room acoustics and design.

In July 2022, Adidas introduced its inaugural collaboration with Spinnova by launching a new product. The Adidas TERREX HS represents the first knitted item that incorporates Spinnova technology. This mid-layer hiking hoodie employs a fabric composition consisting of at least 30% wood-based SPINNOVA fibers (alongside other fibers) and 70% organic cotton. The SPINNOVA fibers are derived from wood pulp, ground with water into a paste, and spun into textile fibers. This collaboration marks Adidas' foray into utilizing sustainable materials and advancing innovative manufacturing processes in their product offerings.

In May 2021, Microsoft developed and implemented Brookdale, a prototyping system for wearable technology. This system introduces plug-and-play hardware and user-friendly drag-and-drop software, empowering fashion designers to program and incorporate new technologies into their creations, thus bridging the gap between fashion and technology.

### Global Smart Textiles Market Dynamics

#### DRIVER: Expanding wearable electronics industry

Wearable electronics are driving the growth of the smart textiles market. [Wearable technology](https://www.marketsandmarkets.com/Market-Reports/wearable-electronics-market-983.html) adoption has gained traction over the last few years due to increasing consumer demand for health, fitness, and networking. Smart textiles, which contain electronic components (sensors, actuators, conductive fibres), enable wearable technology to be lighter, more flexible, and more wearable. Smart textiles are now beginning to find their place in fitness clothing, medical clothing, military uniforms, and professional athletic clothing. They provide real-time data about user biometric parameters, heart rate, temperature, and movement. Consumer and industry demand for multifunctional wearables is increasing, leading to collaboration between textile manufacturers and electronics firms to deliver smarter, more integrated solutions. The rapid convergence of IoT and AI technology into personal health monitoring and smart clothing contributes to the importance of smart-dominated textiles in wearable electronics. The increasing miniaturization of sensors and energy harvesting devices will further foster the development of intelligent functions in clothing that cost less and simultaneously remain comfortable. Moreover, the demand for fashionable, functional, connected clothing will continue to drive the industry. Even a modest increase in the wearable electronics sector's size should have a more pronounced influence on the growth of the overall smart textiles sector, while acknowledging the different component subsectors that make it up.

#### RESTRAINTS: Lack of standards and regulations

The absence of universally accepted standards and regulatory structures is a key constraint inhibiting the growth of the smart textiles sector. Smart textiles typically integrate elements of conventional textiles, electronics, and digital technology; thus, interoperability, safety, and quality assurance fall outside of an industry-standardized way of working, and manufacturers are left to develop their own way of doing things. For example, small and mid-sized manufacturers may find it difficult to legislate assumptions about compliance due to inconsistencies or region-specific compliance requirements and regulations, but the situation is more limited for other forms of organizations, too. A large-scale adoption of smart textile products is particularly inhibited in those sectors operating in healthcare and the military, where legitimacy, reliability, and safety for many applications are necessary. The differences in performance metrics, testing protocols, or durability benchmarks for e-textiles among international bodies make cross-border trade and innovation slow and cumbersome. The combination of textiles and electronics is problematic for intellectual property protections, which may also be inhibiting investment. Demand for modification increases manufacturing cost and potentially increases development time related to variations in markets and regulatory regimes. For the smart textiles industry to develop and grow effectively, collaborative efforts from the industry, associations, governments, and standardization bodies on a global level are critical. Creating common protocols and certification pathways for smart textile products will lead to more reliability and trust from users, thus simplifying commercialization and entry into markets globally.

#### OPPORTUNITY: Development of multi-featured and hybrid smart textiles

The emergence of hybrid smart textiles with multiple features also provides opportunities for advancing innovation and market activity. As consumer and industrial needs and trends change, the demand for adaptable textiles will increasingly require textiles to be more than sensing and responsive. In addition to sensing and responsive capabilities, demand is increasing for adaptable textiles with multiple functions such as energy harvesting, wireless communication, thermal comfort, antimicrobial attributes, etc. Hybrid smart textiles that integrate many technologies such as nanomaterials, shape memory polymers, and microelectronic circuits, can develop wider-ranging and more sophisticated applications. For example, smart garments can be developed in a healthcare environment that integrates multi-sensory capabilities to monitor vitals, detect pressure ulcers, and simultaneously deliver therapeutic stimuli. In military applications, design initiatives to develop multifunctional uniforms will assist the soldier in adapting to environmental demands while also allowing biometric data collection to monitor a soldier's health. In sports and fitness applications, hybrid wearables paired with smart textiles can provide real-time feedback, correction of posture, and monitoring of hydration levels, etc. R&D investments and interdisciplinary collaborations between materials science, electronics, and textile engineering groups are facilitating these developments. For example, hospitals are currently evaluating the use of integrated smart textiles with off-bed sensing that allows monitoring of bedsheets and mattresses. The rapid advancements in integrating artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning with smart textile systems promise to provide greater personalisation associated with user feedback and adaptive or appropriate movements, contributing to the value proposition.

#### CHALLENGE: High production and retail price

The high production and retail price is perhaps the single largest barrier to widespread smart textile use. Synthesizing multiple design and manufacturing processes involving sensors, actuators, advanced conductive materials, and wireless communication systems, in flexible and fabric structures, requires non-traditional and complex manufacturing processes using alternative materials and textiles, which raises their costs heavily. Also, lots of smart textiles will still require more advanced R&D, prototyping, and testing to verify that the smart textile will function or outlast traditional textiles regarding wearability, appearance, and comfort, which adds to the total cost. Smart medical wearables or smart army-based uniforms have very precise requirements involving significant performance specifications to ensure accurate measures and reportable productivity measures, which would be expensive to design, produce, and price. Also, many smart textiles are still not fully capable of being scaled to work with typical production capacities and are not universally accepted as mass-produced fast fashion, leading to low yield efficiencies and longer production times. Neither the industries nor consumers are willing to accept the risks and therefore costs due to the cost-benefit ratio of smart textiles, and that's assuming smart features cannot be substituted with smart watches and mobile apps; but cost would remove them as most of the public are increasingly price sensitive.

The smart textiles market ecosystem is a robust and evolving network of leading manufacturers, technology innovators, and diverse end-user verticals. For instance, smart garments enable real-time patient monitoring in healthcare, while intelligent textiles improve cabin comfort and safety in the automotive sector. The convergence of electronics, data analytics, and textile manufacturing underpins this ecosystem, fostering cross-industry collaboration. As adoption rises, this integrated value chain will be pivotal in scaling solutions and unlocking new commercial opportunities.

#### Active/Ultra type smart textiles segment is likely to register the highest CAGR during the forecast period.

The Active/Ultra Smart Textiles segment is expected to register the highest CAGR during the forecast period due to its advanced functionality, increasing use cases, and rapid technological innovation. Unlike passive and semi-smart textiles, active smart textiles not only sense external stimuli but also respond or adapt in real-time using actuators, embedded electronics, or shape memory materials. This dynamic functionality is driving their adoption across high-growth sectors such as healthcare, defense, sports, and fashion. In healthcare, for instance, ultra-smart garments can administer stimuli, deliver drugs, or adjust to changing body conditions—providing real-time intervention, not just monitoring. In defense, these textiles can detect environmental threats and respond by changing color or temperature, enhancing soldier safety and survivability. Additionally, the rise of AI and IoT integration in wearables is further boosting the capabilities of active smart textiles, making them more responsive, personalized, and intelligent. The growing focus on performance optimization in sportswear and fashion-tech collaborations is also creating new commercial opportunities. As consumers and industries demand more interactive, multifunctional, and adaptive clothing, the technological edge and high innovation potential of active smart textiles position them as the fastest-growing segment in the smart textile market landscape.

#### By Application, the sensing segment is expected to account for the largest market share in 2025.

The sensing application segment is expected to account for a large share of the smart textiles market by 2025, indicating a growing demand for real-time data collection across different industry sectors. Sensing smart textiles have pressure, movement, temperature, or biometric sensors to collect data built into the textile without bulky hardware. In sensing smart textiles, demand is particularly high in healthcare sectors for patient tracking, sports and fitness for assessing performance, and the military for situational awareness, risk regulation around a task, and general safety. As wearable technologies increase engagement with life on a day-to-day basis, sensing textiles offer a suitable way to facilitate monitoring of both the body and its environment. The non-invasive characteristics of these textiles are a desirable feature promoting continuous use, especially on textile garments (e.g., shirts, socks) that don't bother the user in continuous measurement of heart rate, posture, respiration, and muscle output. Key developments expanding the potential of sensing smart textiles are embroidery and fabric development through conductive yarns, embedding developable flexible electronics, and integrating machine learning to take greater advantage of sensors. With the growth of the Internet of Things (IoT), demand is expected for reliable sensors to be placed on the body that communicate wirelessly to connected devices. The priority for many of the key players in the industry is to develop minute sensors developed to move with the intended use of the textiles while also creating durable fabrics to support continuous data collection over a number of intervals in daily use.

By 2024, the market saw sustainable growth, primarily fueled by demand for functional fabrics. From 2025 to 2033, it anticipates a CAGR of 6.5%. The rise of Industry 4.0, incorporating digital technologies into production, is a major growth driver. This integration enhances productivity and quality through automated defect detection, precision weaving, and digital pattern customization.

The smart textile manufacturing system market is witnessing a notable evolution, driven by the integration of technologies such as sensors, robotics, AI, IoT, and CAD. These systems produce smart textiles designed for sectors like healthcare, military, sportswear, fashion, automotive, and home furnishings. Smart systems facilitate efficient and precise textile production with capabilities like fiber integration, electronic embedding, automated pattern recognition, thermal regulation, and real-time monitoring.

The adoption of smart wearables in healthcare and sports offers significant opportunities. Hospitals and wellness centers are utilizing smart garments for continuous monitoring and remote diagnostics, necessitating precise manufacturing techniques for embedded sensors and responsive fabrics. In sports, the demand for garments providing real-time tracking and muscle stimulation propels the market.

However, challenges like high capital investment and operational complexity persist. The cost of equipment, infrastructure, and workforce training can deter small enterprises. Regions with low labor costs also find it challenging due to the need for skilled technical workers and retrofitting legacy systems.

The lack of standardized protocols and interoperability among components is a critical hurdle. Smart textiles involve integrating sensors, microcontrollers, and conductive threads, which must work harmoniously without compromising performance and safety. Manufacturers often face issues with material compatibility and signal transmission, calling for industry collaboration to establish unified guidelines.

In terms of technology, the market is segmented into electronic-based smart textiles, nanotechnology-enabled textiles, and hybrid systems. In 2024, electronic-based textiles led the market due to their established presence in commercial applications. However, nanotechnology is poised for rapid growth due to its potential for molecular-level enhancements, including thermal regulation and antibacterial properties.

By manufacturing process, fabric weaving/knitting systems generated the highest revenue in 2024. These are essential for integrating conductive threads and sensors during production. Yet, the domain of automation and robotics is set to witness the highest CAGR, enhancing efficiency and ensuring zero-defect manufacturing.

Geographically, North America dominated the market revenue share in 2024 due to its advanced manufacturing ecosystem and demand for wearable tech. Europe followed with strong public-private R&D frameworks, especially in technical textiles. The Asia Pacific region is expected to achieve the highest CAGR, driven by smart factory investments and government-backed programs in countries like China, Japan, South Korea, and India.

The competitive landscape in 2024 involved active innovation, cross-sector collaboration, and global supply chain diversification. Key players like DuPont and Gentherm invested in material science integration, while others like Karl Mayer Group expanded smart machine offerings. Moving forward, companies will focus on modular platforms, AI-enhanced quality systems, and developing regional partnerships.

For the period 2023 to 2033, this analysis considers multiple segments and geographies, focusing on technological advancements and market strategies that drive smart textile production. The forecast highlights significant opportunities and challenges, suggesting a dynamic landscape poised for growth and transformation in the coming decade.
